{"content": "     1|"""
     2|MCP Server Management CLI — ``hermes mcp`` subcommand.
     3|
     4|Implements ``hermes mcp add/remove/list/test/configure`` for interactive
     5|MCP server lifecycle management (issue #690 Phase 2).
     6|
     7|Relies on tools/mcp_tool.py for connection/discovery and keeps
     8|configuration in ~/.hermes/config.yaml under the ``mcp_servers`` key.
     9|"""
    10|
    11|import asyncio
    12|import logging
    13|import os
    14|import re
    15|import time
    16|from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
    17|
    18|from hermes_cli.config import (
    19|    cfg_get,
    20|    load_config,
    21|    save_config,
    22|    get_env_value,
    23|    save_env_value,
    24|    get_hermes_home,  # noqa: F401 — used by test mocks
    25|)
    26|from hermes_cli.colors import Colors, color
    27|from hermes_constants import display_hermes_home
    28|from tools.mcp_tool import _ENV_VAR_PATTERN
    29|
    30|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    31|
    32|_ENV_VAR_NAME_RE = re.compile(r"^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$")
    33|
    34|
    35|_MCP_PRESETS: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]] = {
    36|    "codex": {
    37|        "command": "codex",
    38|        "args": ["mcp-server"],
    39|    },
    40|}
    41|
    42|
    43|# ─── UI Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    44|
    45|def _info(text: str):
    46|    print(color(f"  {text}", Colors.DIM))
    47|
    48|def _success(text: str):
    49|    print(color(f"  ✓ {text}", Colors.GREEN))
    50|
    51|def _warning(text: str):
    52|    print(color(f"  ⚠ {text}", Colors.YELLOW))
    53|
    54|def _error(text: str):
    55|    print(color(f"  ✗ {text}", Colors.RED))
    56|
    57|
    58|def _confirm(question: str, default: bool = True) -> bool:
    59|    default_str = "Y/n" if default else "y/N"
    60|    try:
    61|        val = input(color(f"  {question} [{default_str}]: ", Colors.YELLOW)).strip().lower()
    62|    except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
    63|        print()
    64|        return default
    65|    if not val:
    66|        return default
    67|    return val in {"y", "yes"}
    68|
    69|
    70|def _prompt(question: str, *, password: bool = False, default: str = "") -> str:
    71|    from hermes_cli.cli_output import prompt as _shared_prompt
    72|    return _shared_prompt(question, default=default, password=password)
    73|
    74|
    75|# ─── Config Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    76|
    77|def _get_mcp_servers(config: Optional[dict] = None) -> Dict[str, dict]:
    78|    """Return the ``mcp_servers`` dict from config, or empty dict."""
    79|    if config is None:
    80|        config = load_config()
    81|    servers = config.get("mcp_servers")
    82|    if not servers or not isinstance(servers, dict):
    83|        return {}
    84|    return servers
    85|
    86|
    87|def _save_mcp_server(name: str, server_config: dict):
    88|    """Add or update a server entry in config.yaml."""
    89|    config = load_config()
    90|    config.setdefault("mcp_servers", {})[name] = server_config
    91|    save_config(config)
    92|
    93|
    94|def _remove_mcp_server(name: str) -> bool:
    95|    """Remove a server from config.yaml.  Returns True if it existed."""
    96|    config = load_config()
    97|    servers = config.get("mcp_servers", {})
    98|    if name not in servers:
    99|        return False
   100|    del servers[name]
   101|    if not servers:
   102|        config.pop("mcp_servers", None)
   103|    save_config(config)
   104|    return True
   105|
   106|
   107|def _env_key_for_server(name: str) -> str:
   108|    """Convert server name to an env-var key like ``MCP_MYSERVER_API_KEY``."""
   109|    return f"MCP_{name.upper().replace('-', '_')}_API_KEY"
   110|
   111|
   112|def _parse_env_assignments(raw_env: Optional[List[str]]) -> Dict[str, str]:
   113|    """Parse ``KEY=VALUE`` strings from CLI args into an env dict."""
   114|    parsed: Dict[str, str] = {}
   115|    for item in raw_env or []:
   116|        text = str(item or "").strip()
   117|        if not text:
   118|            continue
   119|        if "=" not in text:
   120|            raise ValueError(f"Invalid --env value '{text}' (expected KEY=VALUE)")
   121|        key, value = text.split("=", 1)
   122|        key = key.strip()
   123|        if not key:
   124|            raise ValueError(f"Invalid --env value '{text}' (missing variable name)")
   125|        if not _ENV_VAR_NAME_RE.match(key):
   126|            raise ValueError(f"Invalid --env variable name '{key}'")
   127|        parsed[key] = value
   128|    return parsed
   129|
   130|
   131|def _apply_mcp_preset(
   132|    name: str,
   133|    *,
   134|    preset_name: Optional[str],
   135|    url: Optional[str],
   136|    command: Optional[str],
   137|    cmd_args: List[str],
   138|    server_config: Dict[str, Any],
   139|) -> tuple[Optional[str], Optional[str], List[str], bool]:
   140|    """Apply a known MCP preset when transport details were omitted."""
   141|    if not preset_name:
   142|        return url, command, cmd_args, False
   143|
   144|    preset = _MCP_PRESETS.get(preset_name)
   145|    if not preset:
   146|        raise ValueError(f"Unknown MCP preset: {preset_name}")
   147|
   148|    if url or command:
   149|        return url, command, cmd_args, False
   150|
   151|    url = preset.get("url")
   152|    command = preset.get("command")
   153|    cmd_args = list(preset.get("args") or [])
   154|
   155|    if url:
   156|        server_config["url"] = url
   157|    if command:
   158|        server_config["command"] = command
   159|    if cmd_args:
   160|        server_config["args"] = cmd_args
   161|
   162|    return url, command, cmd_args, True
   163|
   164|
   165|# ─── Discovery (temporary connect) ───────────────────────────────────────────
   166|
   167|def _probe_single_server(
   168|    name: str, config: dict, connect_timeout: float = 30
   169|) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
   170|    """Temporarily connect to one MCP server, list its tools, disconnect.
   171|
   172|    Returns list of ``(tool_name, description)`` tuples.
   173|    Raises on connection failure.
   174|    """
   175|    from tools.mcp_tool import (
   176|        _ensure_mcp_loop,
   177|        _run_on_mcp_loop,
   178|        _connect_server,
   179|        _stop_mcp_loop,
   180|    )
   181|
   182|    _ensure_mcp_loop()
   183|
   184|    tools_found: List[Tuple[str, str]] = []
   185|
   186|    async def _probe():
   187|        server = await asyncio.wait_for(
   188|            _connect_server(name, config), timeout=connect_timeout
   189|        )
   190|        for t in server._tools:
   191|            desc = getattr(t, "description", "") or ""
   192|            # Truncate long descriptions for display
   193|            if len(desc) > 80:
   194|                desc = desc[:77] + "..."
   195|            tools_found.append((t.name, desc))
   196|        await server.shutdown()
   197|
   198|    try:
   199|        _run_on_mcp_loop(_probe(), timeout=connect_timeout + 10)
   200|    except BaseException as exc:
   201|        raise _unwrap_exception_group(exc) from None
   202|    finally:
   203|        _stop_mcp_loop()
   204|
   205|    return tools_found
   206|
   207|
   208|def _unwrap_exception_group(exc: BaseException) -> Exception:
   209|    """Extract the root-cause exception from anyio TaskGroup wrappers.
   210|
   211|    The MCP SDK uses anyio task groups, which wrap errors in
   212|    ``BaseExceptionGroup`` / ``ExceptionGroup``.  This makes error
   213|    messages opaque ("unhandled errors in a TaskGroup").  We unwrap
   214|    to surface the real cause (e.g. "401 Unauthorized").
   215|    """
   216|    while isinstance(exc, BaseExceptionGroup) and exc.exceptions:
   217|        exc = exc.exceptions[0]
   218|    # Return a plain Exception so callers can catch normally
   219|    if isinstance(exc, Exception):
   220|        return exc
   221|    return RuntimeError(str(exc))
   222|
   223|
   224|# ─── hermes mcp add ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   225|
   226|def cmd_mcp_add(args):
   227|    """Add a new MCP server with discovery-first tool selection."""
   228|    name = args.name
   229|    url = getattr(args, "url", None)
   230|    # Read from `mcp_command` (set by --command via explicit dest) — see
   231|    # mcp_add_p.add_argument("--command", dest="mcp_command", ...) in
   232|    # hermes_cli/main.py for why the dest is renamed.
   233|    command = getattr(args, "mcp_command", None)
   234|    cmd_args = getattr(args, "args", None) or []
   235|    auth_type = getattr(args, "auth", None)
   236|    preset_name = getattr(args, "preset", None)
   237|    raw_env = getattr(args, "env", None)
   238|
   239|    server_config: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   240|    try:
   241|        explicit_env = _parse_env_assignments(raw_env)
   242|        url, command, cmd_args, _preset_applied = _apply_mcp_preset(
   243|            name,
   244|            preset_name=preset_name,
   245|            url=url,
   246|            command=command,
   247|            cmd_args=list(cmd_args),
   248|            server_config=server_config,
   249|        )
   250|    except ValueError as exc:
   251|        _error(str(exc))
   252|        return
   253|
   254|    if url and explicit_env:
   255|        _error("--env is only supported for stdio MCP servers (--command or stdio presets)")
   256|        return
   257|
   258|    # Validate transport
   259|    if not url and not command:
   260|        _error("Must specify --url <endpoint>, --command <cmd>, or --preset <name>")

   261|        _info("Examples:")

   266|
   267|    # Check if server already exists
   268|    existing = _get_mcp_servers()
   269|    if name in existing:
   270|        if not _confirm(f"Server '{name}' already exists. Overwrite?", default=False):
   271|            _info("Cancelled.")
   272|            return
   273|
   274|    # Build initial config
   275|    if url:
   276|        server_config["url"] = url
   277|    else:
   278|        server_config["command"] = command
   279|        if cmd_args:
   280|            server_config["args"] = cmd_args

   282|            server_config["env"] = explicit_env
   283|
   284|
   285|    # ── Authentication ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   286|
   287|    if url and auth_type == "oauth":
   288|        print()
   289|        _info(f"Starting OAuth flow for '{name}'...")
   290|        oauth_ok = False
   291|        try:
   292|            from tools.mcp_oauth_manager import get_manager
   293|            oauth_auth = get_manager().get_or_build_provider(name, url, None)
   294|            if oauth_auth:
   295|                server_config["auth"] = "oauth"
   296|                _success("OAuth configured (tokens will be acquired on first connection)")
   297|                oauth_ok=True
   298|            else:
   299|                _warning("OAuth setup failed — MCP SDK auth module not available")
   300|        except Exception as exc:
   301|            _warning(f"OAuth error: {exc}")
   302|
   303|        if not oauth_ok:
   304|            _info("This server may not support OAuth.")
   305|            if _confirm("Continue without authentication?", default=True):
   306|                # Don't store auth: oauth — server doesn't support it
   307|                pass
   308|            else:
   309|                _info("Cancelled.")
   310|                return
   311|
   312|    elif url:
   313|        # Prompt for API key / Bearer token for HTTP servers
   314|        print()
   315|        _info(f"Connecting to {url}")
   316|        needs_auth = _confirm("Does this server require authentication?", default=True)
   317|        if needs_auth:
   318|            if auth_type == "header" or not auth_type:
   319|                env_key = _env_key_for_server(name)
   320|                existing_key = get_env_value(env_key)
   321|                if existing_key:
   322|                    _success(f"{env_key}: already configured")
   323|                    api_key = existing_key
   324|                else:
   325|                    api_key = _prompt("API key / Bearer token", password=True)
   326|                    if api_key:
   327|                        save_env_value(env_key, api_key)
   328|                        _success(f"Saved to {display_hermes_home()}/.env as {env_key}")
   329|
   330|                # Set header with env var interpolation
   331|                if api_key or existing_key:
   332|                    server_config["headers"] = {
   333|                        "Authorization": f"Bearer ${{{env_key}}}"
   334|                    }
   335|
   336|    # ── Discovery: connect and list tools ─────────────────────────────
   337|
   338|    print()
   339|    print(color(f"  Connecting to '{name}'...", Colors.CYAN))
   340|
   341|    try:
   342|        tools = _probe_single_server(name, server_config)
   343|    except Exception as exc:
   344|        _error(f"Failed to connect: {exc}")
   345|        if _confirm("Save config anyway (you can test later)?", default=False):
   346|            server_config["enabled"] = False
   347|            _save_mcp_server(name, server_config)
   348|            _success(f"Saved '{name}' to config (disabled)")
   349|            _info("Fix the issue, then: hermes mcp test " + name)
   350|        return
   351|
   352|    if not tools:
   353|        _warning("Server connected but reported no tools.")
   354|        if _confirm("Save config anyway?", default=True):
   355|            _save_mcp_server(name, server_config)
   356|            _success(f"Saved '{name}' to config")
   357|        return
   358|
   359|    # ── Tool selection ────────────────────────────────────────────────
   360|
   361|    print()
   362|    _success(f"Connected! Found {len(tools)} tool(s) from '{name}':")
   363|    print()
   364|    for tool_name, desc in tools:
   365|        short = desc[:60] + "..." if len(desc) > 60 else desc
   366|        print(f"    {color(tool_name, Colors.GREEN):40s} {short}")
   367|    print()
   368|
   369|    # Ask: enable all, select, or cancel
   370|    try:
   371|        choice = input(
   372|            color(f"  Enable all {len(tools)} tools? [Y/n/select]: ", Colors.YELLOW)
   373|        ).strip().lower()
   374|    except (KeyboardInterrupt, EOFError):
   375|        print()
   376|        _info("Cancelled.")
   377|        return
   378|
   379|    if choice in {"n", "no"}:
   380|        _info("Cancelled — server not saved.")
   381|        return
   382|
   383|    if choice in {"s", "select"}:
   384|        # Interactive tool selection
   385|        from hermes_cli.curses_ui import curses_checklist
   386|
   387|        labels = [f"{t[0]}  —  {t[1]}" for t in tools]
   388|        pre_selected = set(range(len(tools)))
   389|
   390|        chosen = curses_checklist(
   391|            f"Select tools for '{name}'",
   392|            labels,
   393|            pre_selected,
   394|        )
   395|
   396|        if not chosen:
   397|            _info("No tools selected — server not saved.")
   398|            return
   399|
   400|        chosen_names = [tools[i][0] for i in sorted(chosen)]
   401|        server_config.setdefault("tools", {})["include"] = chosen_names
   402|
   403|        tool_count = len(chosen_names)
   404|        total = len(tools)
   405|    else:
   406|        # Enable all (no filter needed — default behaviour)
   407|        tool_count = len(tools)
   408|        total = len(tools)
   409|
   410|    # ── Save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   411|
   412|    server_config["enabled"] = True
   413|    _save_mcp_server(name, server_config)
   414|
   415|    print()
   416|    _success(f"Saved '{name}' to {display_hermes_home()}/config.yaml ({tool_count}/{total} tools enabled)")
   417|    _info("Start a new session to use these tools.")
   418|
   419|
   420|# ─── hermes mcp remove ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   421|
   422|def cmd_mcp_remove(args):
   423|    """Remove an MCP server from config."""
   424|    name = args.name

   432|        return
   433|
   434|    if not _confirm(f"Remove server '{name}'?", default=True):
   435|        _info("Cancelled.")
   436|        return
   437|
   438|    _remove_mcp_server(name)

I tried to connect Composio.

What I did:
- Confirmed Hermes has a `composio` MCP server entry.
- Repaired the config so it points to:
  `https://connect.composio.dev/mcp`
- Set auth mode to OAuth.
- Tried `hermes mcp login composio`.

Blocker:
- Composio is returning a Cloudflare challenge / 403 from this WSL environment before Hermes can start OAuth.
- Because of that, I can’t get the Google authorization link for Gmail, Calendar, Tasks, or Drive yet.

Current config is ready here:
`/opt/data/profiles/sitekick/config.yaml`

Next best option:
1. Try Composio login from your regular Windows browser / terminal environment, or
2. Use direct Google OAuth instead of Composio so I can connect Calendar/Gmail/Tasks/Drive locally.

Recommended: use direct Google OAuth for now so I can check your calendar today without waiting on the Composio Cloudflare block.

messages.name: read_file
messages.content:
{"content": "     1|#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2|"""
     3|MCP (Model Context Protocol) Client Support
     4|
     5|Connects to external MCP servers via stdio, HTTP/StreamableHTTP, or SSE
     6|transport, discovers their tools, and registers them into the hermes-agent
     7|tool registry so the agent can call them like any built-in tool.
     8|
     9|Configuration is read from ~/.hermes/config.yaml under the ``mcp_servers`` key.
    10|The ``mcp`` Python package is optional -- if not installed, this module is a
    11|no-op and logs a debug message.
    12|
    13|Example config::
    14|
    15|    mcp_servers:
    16|      filesystem:
    17|        command: "npx"
    18|        args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem", "/tmp"]
    19|        env: {}
    20|        timeout: 120         # per-tool-call timeout in seconds (default: 120)
    21|        connect_timeout: 60  # initial connection timeout (default: 60)
    22|      github:
    23|        command: "npx"
    24|        args: ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-github"]
    25|        env:
    26|          GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN: "ghp_..."
    27|        supports_parallel_tool_calls: true  # tools from this server may run concurrently
    28|      remote_api:
    29|        url: "https://my-mcp-server.example.com/mcp"
    30|        headers:
    31|          Authorization: "Bearer sk-..."
    32|        timeout: 180
    33|      searxng:
    34|        url: "http://localhost:8000/sse"
    35|        transport: sse       # use SSE transport instead of Streamable HTTP
    36|        timeout: 180
    37|        connect_timeout: 10
    38|        command: "npx"
    39|        args: ["-y", "analysis-server"]
    40|        sampling:                    # server-initiated LLM requests
    41|          enabled: true              # default: true
    42|          model: "gemini-3-flash"    # override model (optional)
    43|          max_tokens_cap: 4096       # max tokens per request
    44|          timeout: 30                # LLM call timeout (seconds)
    45|          max_rpm: 10                # max requests per minute
    46|          allowed_models: []         # model whitelist (empty = all)
    47|          max_tool_rounds: 5         # tool loop limit (0 = disable)
    48|          log_level: "info"          # audit verbosity
    49|
    50|Features:
    51|    - Stdio transport (command + args) and HTTP/StreamableHTTP transport (url)
    52|    - SSE transport (transport: sse) for MCP servers using the SSE protocol
    53|    - Automatic reconnection with exponential backoff (up to 5 retries)
    54|    - Environment variable filtering for stdio subprocesses (security)
    55|    - Credential stripping in error messages returned to the LLM
    56|    - Configurable per-server timeouts for tool calls and connections
    57|    - Thread-safe architecture with dedicated background event loop
    58|    - Sampling support: MCP servers can request LLM completions via
    59|      sampling/createMessage (text and tool-use responses)
    60|    - Parallel tool call opt-in: per-server ``supports_parallel_tool_calls``
    61|      flag allows concurrent execution of tools from the same server
    62|
    63|Architecture:
    64|    A dedicated background event loop (_mcp_loop) runs in a daemon thread.
    65|    Each MCP server runs as a long-lived asyncio Task on this loop, keeping
    66|    its transport context alive. Tool call coroutines are scheduled onto the
    67|    loop via ``run_coroutine_threadsafe()``.
    68|
    69|    On shutdown, each server Task is signalled to exit its ``async with``
    70|    block, ensuring the anyio cancel-scope cleanup happens in the *same*
    71|    Task that opened the connection (required by anyio).
    72|
    73|Thread safety:
    74|    _servers and _mcp_loop/_mcp_thread are accessed from both the MCP
    75|    background thread and caller threads.  All mutations are protected by
    76|    _lock so the code is safe regardless of GIL presence (e.g. Python 3.13+
    77|    free-threading).
    78|"""
    79|
    80|import asyncio
    81|import concurrent.futures
    82|import inspect
    83|import json
    84|import logging
    85|import math
    86|import os
    87|import re
    88|import shutil
    89|import sys
    90|import threading
    91|import time
    92|from datetime import datetime
    93|from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    94|
    95|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    96|
    97|
    98|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    99|# Stdio subprocess stderr redirection
   100|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   101|#
   102|# The MCP SDK's ``stdio_client(server, errlog=sys.stderr)`` defaults the
   103|# subprocess stderr stream to the parent process's real stderr, i.e. the
   104|# user's TTY.  That means any MCP server we spawn at startup (FastMCP
   105|# banners, slack-mcp-server JSON startup logs, etc.) writes directly onto
   106|# the terminal while prompt_toolkit / Rich is rendering the TUI — which
   107|# corrupts the display and can hang the session.
   108|#
   109|# Instead we redirect every stdio MCP subprocess's stderr into a shared
   110|# per-profile log file (~/.hermes/logs/mcp-stderr.log), tagged with the
   111|# server name so individual servers remain debuggable.
   112|#
   113|# Fallback is os.devnull if opening the log file fails for any reason.
   114|
   115|_mcp_stderr_log_fh: Optional[Any] = None
   116|_mcp_stderr_log_lock = threading.Lock()
   117|
   118|
   119|def _get_mcp_stderr_log() -> Any:
   120|    """Return a shared append-mode file handle for MCP subprocess stderr.
   121|
   122|    Opened once per process and reused for every stdio server.  Must have a
   123|    real OS-level file descriptor (``fileno()``) because asyncio's subprocess
   124|    machinery wires the child's stderr directly to that fd.  Falls back to
   125|    ``/dev/null`` if opening the log file fails.
   126|    """
   127|    global _mcp_stderr_log_fh
   128|    with _mcp_stderr_log_lock:
   129|        if _mcp_stderr_log_fh is not None:
   130|            return _mcp_stderr_log_fh
   131|        try:
   132|            from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
   133|            log_dir = get_hermes_home() / "logs"
   134|            log_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   135|            log_path = log_dir / "mcp-stderr.log"
   136|            # Line-buffered so server output lands on disk promptly; errors=
   137|            # "replace" tolerates garbled binary output from misbehaving
   138|            # servers.
   139|            fh = open(log_path, "a", encoding="utf-8", errors="replace", buffering=1)
   140|            # Sanity-check: confirm a real fd is available before we commit.
   141|            fh.fileno()
   142|            _mcp_stderr_log_fh = fh
   143|        except Exception as exc:  # pragma: no cover — best-effort fallback
   144|            logger.debug("Failed to open MCP stderr log, using devnull: %s", exc)
   145|            try:
   146|                _mcp_stderr_log_fh = open(os.devnull, "w", encoding="utf-8")
   147|            except Exception:
   148|                # Last resort: the real stderr.  Not ideal for TUI users but
   149|                # it matches pre-fix behavior.
   150|                _mcp_stderr_log_fh = sys.stderr
   151|        return _mcp_stderr_log_fh
   152|
   153|
   154|def _write_stderr_log_header(server_name: str) -> None:
   155|    """Write a human-readable session marker before launching a server.
   156|

  2204|    def _handler(args: dict, **kwargs) -> str:
  2205|        # Circuit breaker: if this server has failed too many times
  2206|        # consecutively, short-circuit with a clear message so the model
  2207|        # stops retrying and uses alternative approaches (#10447).
  2208|        #
  2209|        # Once the cooldown elapses, the breaker transitions to
  2210|        # half-open: we let the *next* call through as a probe. On
  2211|        # success the success-path below resets the breaker; on
  2212|        # failure the error paths below bump the count again, which
  2213|        # re-stamps the open-time via _bump_server_error (re-arming
  2214|        # the cooldown).
  2215|        if _server_error_counts.get(server_name, 0) >= _CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD:
  2216|            opened_at = _server_breaker_opened_at.get(server_name, 0.0)
  2217|            age = time.monotonic() - opened_at
  2218|            if age < _CIRCUIT_BREAKER_COOLDOWN_SEC:
  2219|                remaining = max(1, int(_CIRCUIT_BREAKER_COOLDOWN_SEC - age))
  2220|                return json.dumps({
  2221|                    "error": (
  2222|                        f"MCP server '{server_name}' is unreachable after "
  2223|                        f"{_server_error_counts[server_name]} consecutive "
  2224|                        f"failures. Auto-retry available in ~{remaining}s. "
  2225|                        f"Do NOT retry this tool yet — use alternative "
  2226|                        f"approaches or ask the user to check the MCP server."
  2227|                    )
  2228|                }, ensure_ascii=False)

  2252|                }, ensure_ascii=False)
  2253|
  2254|            # Collect text from content blocks. MCP tool results can also
  2255|            # include ImageContent blocks (screenshot / Blockbench / Playwright
  2256|            # etc.); cache those via the gateway's image-cache helper so they
  2257|            # flow through Hermes' MEDIA: tag convention and out to messaging
  2258|            # adapters that render images natively. Without this, image blocks
  2259|            # were silently dropped and the agent got an empty response.
  2260|            #
  2261|            # Distilled from #17915 (c3115644151) and #10848 (gnanirahulnutakki),
  2262|            # both too stale to cherry-pick. #10848's approach (integrate with
  2263|            # Hermes' MEDIA tag + cache_image_from_bytes) was the cleaner of
  2264|            # the two — plugs into existing infrastructure.
  2265|            parts: List[str] = []
  2266|            for block in (result.content or []):
  2267|                if hasattr(block, "text") and block.text:
  2268|                    parts.append(block.text)
  2269|                    continue
  2270|                image_tag = _cache_mcp_image_block(block)
  2271|                if image_tag:
  2272|                    parts.append(image_tag)
  2273|            text_result = "\n".join(parts) if parts else ""
  2274|
  2275|            # Combine content + structuredContent when both are present.
  2276|            # MCP spec: content is model-oriented (text), structuredContent
  2277|            # is machine-oriented (JSON metadata).  For an AI agent, content
  2278|            # is the primary payload; structuredContent supplements it.
  2279|            structured = getattr(result, "structuredContent", None)

  2284|                        "structuredContent": structured,
  2285|                    }, ensure_ascii=False)

  3176|def discover_mcp_tools() -> List[str]:
  3177|    """Entry point: load config, connect to MCP servers, register tools.
  3178|
  3179|    Called from ``model_tools`` after ``discover_builtin_tools()``. Safe to call even when
  3180|    the ``mcp`` package is not installed (returns empty list).
  3181|
  3182|    Idempotent for already-connected servers. If some servers failed on a
  3183|    previous call, only the missing ones are retried.
  3184|
  3185|    Returns:
  3186|        List of all registered MCP tool names.
  3187|    """
  3188|    if not _MCP_AVAILABLE:
  3189|        logger.debug("MCP SDK not available -- skipping MCP tool discovery")
  3190|        return []
  3191|
  3192|    servers = _load_mcp_config()
  3193|    if not servers:
  3194|        logger.debug("No MCP servers configured")
  3195|        return []

  3202|        ]

  3225|def is_mcp_tool_parallel_safe(tool_name: str) -> bool:
  3226|    """Check if an MCP tool belongs to a server that supports parallel tool calls.
  3227|
  3228|    MCP tool names follow the pattern ``mcp_{server}_{tool}``.  This extracts
  3229|    the server component and checks it against the set of servers whose config
  3230|    includes ``supports_parallel_tool_calls: true``.
  3231|
  3232|    Returns False for non-MCP tools or tools from servers without the flag.
  3233|    """
  3234|    if not tool_name.startswith("mcp_"):

  3236|    # Strip the "mcp_" prefix and extract the server name.
  3237|    # Tool names are: mcp_{sanitized_server}_{sanitized_tool}
  3238|    # We need to check all possible server prefixes because the server name
  3239|    # itself may contain underscores after sanitization.
  3240|    rest = tool_name[4:]  # strip "mcp_"
  3241|    with _lock:
  3242|        for server_name in _parallel_safe_servers:
  3243|            if rest.startswith(server_name + "_") and len(rest) > len(server_name) + 1:
  3244|                return True
  3245|    return False

  3052|async def _discover_and_register_server(name: str, config: dict) -> List[str]:

  3081|def register_mcp_servers(servers: Dict[str, dict]) -> List[str]:

  3084|    Idempotent for already-connected server names. Servers with
  3085|    ``enabled: false`` are skipped without disconnecting existing sessions.

  3096|
  3097|    if not servers:
  3098|        logger.debug("No explicit MCP servers provided")
  3099|        return []
  3100|
  3101|    # Only attempt servers that aren't already connected and are enabled
  3102|    # (enabled: false skips the server entirely without removing its config)
  3103|    with _lock:
  3104|        new_servers = {
  3105|            k: v
  3106|            for k, v in servers.items()
  3107|            if k not in _servers and _parse_boolish(v.get("enabled", True), default=True)

  3110|        for srv_name, srv_cfg in servers.items():

  3116|    if not new_servers:

  3118|
  3119|    # Start the background event loop for MCP connections

  3122|    async def _discover_one(name: str, cfg: dict) -> List[str]:

  3126|    async def _discover_all():
  3127|        server_names = list(new_servers.keys())
  3128|        # Connect to all servers in PARALLEL
  3129|        results = await asyncio.gather(
  3130|            *(_discover_one(name, cfg) for name, cfg in new_servers.items()),

messages.content:
{"content": "     1|"""Central registry for all hermes-agent tools.
     2|
     3|Each tool file calls ``registry.register()`` at module level to declare its
     4|schema, handler, toolset membership, and availability check.  ``model_tools.py``
     5|queries the registry instead of maintaining its own parallel data structures.
     6|
     7|Import chain (circular-import safe):
     8|    tools/registry.py  (no imports from model_tools or tool files)
     9|           ^
    10|    tools/*.py  (import from tools.registry at module level)
    11|           ^
    12|    model_tools.py  (imports tools.registry + all tool modules)
    13|           ^
    14|    run_agent.py, cli.py, batch_runner.py, etc.
    15|"""
    16|
    17|import ast
    18|import importlib
    19|import json
    20|import logging
    21|import threading
    22|import time
    23|from pathlib import Path
    24|from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Set
    25|
    26|logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    27|
    28|
    29|def _is_registry_register_call(node: ast.AST) -> bool:
    30|    """Return True when *node* is a ``registry.register(...)`` call expression."""
    31|    if not isinstance(node, ast.Expr) or not isinstance(node.value, ast.Call):
    32|        return False
    33|    func = node.value.func
    34|    return (
    35|        isinstance(func, ast.Attribute)
    36|        and func.attr == "register"
    37|        and isinstance(func.value, ast.Name)
    38|        and func.value.id == "registry"
    39|    )
    40|
    41|
    42|def _module_registers_tools(module_path: Path) -> bool:
    43|    """Return True when the module contains a top-level ``registry.register(...)`` call.
    44|
    45|    Only inspects module-body statements so that helper modules which happen
    46|    to call ``registry.register()`` inside a function are not picked up.
    47|    """
    48|    try:
    49|        source = module_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    50|        tree = ast.parse(source, filename=str(module_path))
    51|    except (OSError, SyntaxError):
    52|        return False
    53|
    54|    return any(_is_registry_register_call(stmt) for stmt in tree.body)
    55|
    56|
    57|def discover_builtin_tools(tools_dir: Optional[Path] = None) -> List[str]:
    58|    """Import built-in self-registering tool modules and return their module names."""
    59|    tools_path = Path(tools_dir) if tools_dir is not None else Path(__file__).resolve().parent
    60|    module_names = [
    61|        f"tools.{path.stem}"
    62|        for path in sorted(tools_path.glob("*.py"))
    63|        if path.name not in {"__init__.py", "registry.py", "mcp_tool.py"}
    64|        and _module_registers_tools(path)
    65|    ]
    66|
    67|    imported: List[str] = []
    68|    for mod_name in module_names:
    69|        try:
    70|            importlib.import_module(mod_name)
    71|            imported.append(mod_name)
    72|        except Exception as e:
    73|            logger.warning("Could not import tool module %s: %s", mod_name, e)
    74|    return imported
    75|
    76|
    77|class ToolEntry:
    78|    """Metadata for a single registered tool."""
    79|
    80|    __slots__ = (
    81|        "name", "toolset", "schema", "handler", "check_fn",
    82|        "requires_env", "is_async", "description", "emoji",
    83|        "max_result_size_chars", "dynamic_schema_overrides",
    84|    )
    85|
    86|    def __init__(self, name, toolset, schema, handler, check_fn,
    87|                 requires_env, is_async, description, emoji,
    88|                 max_result_size_chars=None, dynamic_schema_overrides=None):
    89|        self.name = name
    90|        self.toolset = toolset
    91|        self.schema = schema
    92|        self.handler = handler
    93|        self.check_fn = check_fn
    94|        self.requires_env = requires_env
    95|        self.is_async = is_async
    96|        self.description = description
    97|        self.emoji = emoji
    98|        self.max_result_size_chars = max_result_size_chars
    99|        # Optional zero-arg callable returning a dict of schema overrides
   100|        # applied at get_definitions() time. Use for fields that depend on
   101|        # runtime config (e.g. delegate_task's description must reflect the
   102|        # user's current delegation.max_concurrent_children / max_spawn_depth
   103|        # so the model isn't told the wrong limits). The callable is invoked
   104|        # on every get_definitions() call; results are merged shallow on top
   105|        # of the base schema before the {"type": "function", ...} wrap.
   106|        self.dynamic_schema_overrides = dynamic_schema_overrides
   107|
   108|
   109|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   110|# check_fn TTL cache
   111|#
   112|# check_fn callables like tools/terminal_tool.check_terminal_requirements
   113|# probe external state (Docker daemon, Modal SDK install, playwright binary
   114|# availability). For a long-lived CLI or gateway process, calling them on
   115|# every get_definitions() is pure waste — external state changes on human
   116|# timescales. Cache results for ~30 s so env-var flips via ``hermes tools``
   117|# or live credential file changes propagate within a turn or two without
   118|# requiring any explicit invalidation.
   119|# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   120|
   121|_CHECK_FN_TTL_SECONDS = 30.0
   122|_check_fn_cache: Dict[Callable, tuple[float, bool]] = {}
   123|_check_fn_cache_lock = threading.Lock()
   124|
   125|
   126|def _check_fn_cached(fn: Callable) -> bool:
   127|    """Return bool(fn()), TTL-cached across calls. Swallows exceptions as False."""
   128|    now = time.monotonic()
   129|    with _check_fn_cache_lock:
   130|        cached = _check_fn_cache.get(fn)
   131|        if cached is not None:
   132|            ts, value = cached
   133|            if now - ts < _CHECK_FN_TTL_SECONDS:
   134|                return value
   135|    try:
   136|        value = bool(fn())
   137|    except Exception:
   138|        value = False
   139|    with _check_fn_cache_lock:
   140|        _check_fn_cache[fn] = (now, value)
   141|    return value
   142|
   143|
   144|def invalidate_check_fn_cache() -> None:
   145|    """Drop all cached ``check_fn`` results. Call after config changes that
   146|    affect tool availability (e.g. ``hermes tools enable``)."""
   147|    with _check_fn_cache_lock:
   148|        _check_fn_cache.clear()
   149|
   150|
   151|class ToolRegistry:
   152|    """Singleton registry that collects tool schemas + handlers from tool files."""
   153|
   154|    def __init__(self):
   155|        self._tools: Dict[str, ToolEntry] = {}
   156|        self._toolset_checks: Dict[str, Callable] = {}
   157|        self._toolset_aliases: Dict[str, str] = {}
   158|        # MCP dynamic refresh can mutate the registry while other threads are
   159|        # reading tool metadata, so keep mutations serialized and readers on
   160|        # stable snapshots.
   161|", "total_lines": 589, "file_size": 24759, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=161 to continue reading (showing 1-160 of 589 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "   160|        # stable snapshots.
   161|        self._lock = threading.RLock()
   162|        # Monotonically-increasing generation counter. Bumped on every
   163|        # mutation (register / deregister / register_toolset_alias / MCP
   164|        # refresh). External callers (e.g. get_tool_definitions) can memoize
   165|        # against it: a cache entry keyed on the generation is valid for as
   166|        # long as the generation hasn't changed.
   167|        self._generation: int = 0
   168|
   169|    def _snapshot_state(self) -> tuple[List[ToolEntry], Dict[str, Callable]]:
   170|        """Return a coherent snapshot of registry entries and toolset checks."""
   171|        with self._lock:
   172|            return list(self._tools.values()), dict(self._toolset_checks)
   173|
   174|    def _snapshot_entries(self) -> List[ToolEntry]:
   175|        """Return a stable snapshot of registered tool entries."""
   176|        return self._snapshot_state()[0]
   177|
   178|    def _snapshot_toolset_checks(self) -> Dict[str, Callable]:
   179|        """Return a stable snapshot of toolset availability checks."""
   180|        return self._snapshot_state()[1]
   181|
   182|    def _evaluate_toolset_check(self, toolset: str, check: Callable | None) -> bool:
   183|        """Run a toolset check, treating missing or failing checks as unavailable/available."""
   184|        if not check:
   185|            return True
   186|        try:
   187|            return bool(check())
   188|        except Exception:
   189|            logger.debug("Toolset %s check raised; marking unavailable", toolset)
   190|            return False
   191|
   192|    def get_entry(self, name: str) -> Optional[ToolEntry]:
   193|        """Return a registered tool entry by name, or None."""
   194|        with self._lock:
   195|            return self._tools.get(name)
   196|
   197|    def get_registered_toolset_names(self) -> List[str]:
   198|        """Return sorted unique toolset names present in the registry."""
   199|        return sorted({entry.toolset for entry in self._snapshot_entries()})
   200|
   201|    def get_tool_names_for_toolset(self, toolset: str) -> List[str]:
   202|        """Return sorted tool names registered under a given toolset."""
   203|        return sorted(
   204|            entry.name for entry in self._snapshot_entries()
   205|            if entry.toolset == toolset
   206|        )
   207|
   208|    def register_toolset_alias(self, alias: str, toolset: str) -> None:
   209|        """Register an explicit alias for a canonical toolset name."""
   210|        with self._lock:
   211|            existing = self._toolset_aliases.get(alias)
   212|            if existing and existing != toolset:
   213|                logger.warning(
   214|                    "Toolset alias collision: '%s' (%s) overwritten by %s",
   215|                    alias, existing, toolset,
   216|                )
   217|            self._toolset_aliases[alias] = toolset
   218|            self._generation += 1
   219|
   220|    def get_registered_toolset_aliases(self) -> Dict[str, str]:
   221|        """Return a snapshot of ``{alias: canonical_toolset}`` mappings."""
   222|        with self._lock:
   223|            return dict(self._toolset_aliases)
   224|
   225|    def get_toolset_alias_target(self, alias: str) -> Optional[str]:
   226|        """Return the canonical toolset name for an alias, or None."""
   227|        with self._lock:
   228|            return self._toolset_aliases.get(alias)
   229|
   230|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   231|    # Registration
   232|    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   233|
   234|    def register(
   235|        self,
   236|        name: str,
   237|        toolset: str,
   238|        schema: dict,
   239|        handler: Callable,
   240|        check_fn: Callable = None,
   241|        requires_env: list = None,
   242|        is_async: bool = False,
   243|        description: str = "",
   244|        emoji: str = "",
   245|        max_result_size_chars: int | float | None = None,
   246|        dynamic_schema_overrides: Callable = None,
   247|        override: bool = False,
   248|    ):
   249|        """Register a tool.  Called at module-import time by each tool file.
   250|
   251|        ``override=True`` is an explicit opt-in for plugins that intend to
   252|        replace an existing built-in tool implementation (e.g. swap the
   253|        default browser tool for a headed-Chrome CDP backend). Without it,
   254|        registrations that would shadow an existing tool from a different
   255|        toolset are rejected to prevent accidental overwrites.
   256|        """
   257|        with self._lock:
   258|            existing = self._tools.get(name)
   259|            if existing and existing.toolset != toolset:
   260|                # Allow MCP-to-MCP overwrites (legitimate: server refresh,
   261|                # or two MCP servers with overlapping tool names).
   262|                both_mcp = (
   263|                    existing.toolset.startswith("mcp-")
   264|                    and toolset.startswith("mcp-")
   265|                )
   266|                if both_mcp:
   267|                    logger.debug(
   268|                        "Tool '%s': MCP toolset '%s' overwriting MCP toolset '%s'",
   269|                        name, toolset, existing.toolset,
   270|                    )
   271|                elif override:
   272|                    # Explicit plugin opt-in: replace the existing tool.
   273|                    # Logged at INFO so the override is auditable in agent.log.
   274|                    logger.info(
   275|                        "Tool '%s': toolset '%s' overriding existing toolset '%s' "
   276|                        "(override=True opt-in)",
   277|                        name, toolset, existing.toolset,
   278|                    )
   279|                else:
   280|                    # Reject shadowing — prevent plugins/MCP from overwriting
   281|                    # built-in tools or vice versa.
   282|                    logger.error(
   283|                        "Tool registration REJECTED: '%s' (toolset '%s') would "
   284|                        "shadow existing tool from toolset '%s'. Pass "
   285|                        "override=True to register() if the replacement is "
   286|                        "intentional, or deregister the existing tool first.",
   287|                        name, toolset, existing.toolset,
   288|                    )
   289|                    return
   290|            self._tools[name] = ToolEntry(
   291|                name=name,
   292|                toolset=toolset,
   293|                schema=schema,
   294|                handler=handler,
   295|                check_fn=check_fn,
   296|                requires_env=requires_env or [],
   297|                is_async=is_async,
   298|                description=description or schema.get("description", ""),
   299|                emoji=emoji,
   300|", "total_lines": 589, "file_size": 24759, "truncated": true, "hint": "Use offset=300 to continue reading (showing 160-299 of 589 lines)", "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

retain minimal fields only).\\\",\\\"[Required] [Step 4]: Confirm spreadsheet connectivity using GOOGLESHEETS_SEARCH_SPREADSHEETS (probe with very small result size; ignore extra metadata).\\\",\\\"[Required] [Step 5]: Confirm task-list connectivity using GOOGLETASKS_LIST_TASK_LISTS (success implies connected even if empty).\\\",\\\"[Optional (if validating mailbox read scope beyond profile)] [Next Step]: List a tiny sample using GMAIL_FETCH_EMAILS (small max_results; function.name: execute_code
function.arguments:
{"code":"import json
from tools.mcp_tool import discover_mcp_tools
from tools.registry import registry

discover_mcp_tools()
def call(tool,args):
    entry=registry.get_entry('mcp_composio_'+tool)
    out=entry.handler(args)
    print(out[:15000])
    return out
call('COMPOSIO_GET_TOOL_SCHEMAS', {'tool_slugs':['GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST','GOOGLECALENDAR_LIST_CALENDARS','GOOGLETASKS_LIST_TASK_LISTS'], 'include':['input_schema','output_schema']})
"}

For example, querying '2026-01-19T00:00:00Z' to '2026-01-20T00:00:00Z' on a calendar in America/Los_Angeles (UTC-8) covers 2026-01-18 4pm to 2026-01-19 4pm local time, potentially missing events on the intended local date. To query for a specific local date, use timestamps with the appropriate timezone offset in timeMin/timeMax (e.g., '2026-01-19T00:00:00-08:00' for PST).\\\",\\\"input_schema\\\":{\\\"type\\\":\\\"object\\\",\\\"properties\\\":{\\\"q\\\":{\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"Free text search terms to find events that match these terms in various fields. The action automatically sets singleEvents=true when orderBy='startTime'.\\\"},\\\"timeMax\\\":{\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"Upper bound (exclusive) for an event's start time to filter by. Milliseconds may be provided but are ignored. If timeMin is set, timeMax must be greater than timeMin. TIMEZONE WARNING: If using UTC times (ending in 'Z') but the calendar is in a different timezone, the time window may not align with local calendar dates. For example, '2026-01-19T00:00:00Z' to '2026-01-20T00:00:00Z' covers 2026-01-18 4pm to 2026-01-19 4pm in America/Los_Angeles (UTC-8). To query a specific local date, use timestamps with the appropriate local timezone offset (e.g., '2026-01-19T00:00:00-08:00' for PST). NOTE: Natural language expressions like 'today', 'tomorrow', 'next week' are NOT supported.\\\"},\\\"timeMin\\\":{\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"Lower bound (exclusive) for an event's end time to filter by. To query a specific local date, use timestamps with the appropriate local timezone offset (e.g., '2026-01-19T00:00:00-08:00' for PST). NOTE: Natural language expressions like 'today', 'tomorrow', 'next week' are NOT supported.\\\"},\\\"timeZone\\\":{\\\"type\\\":\\\"string\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"Time zone used in the response for formatting event times. NOTE: This parameter only affects how event times are displayed in the response. It does NOT change how timeMin/timeMax filtering is interpreted. {"code":"import json
from tools.mcp_tool import discover_mcp_tools
from tools.registry import registry

discover_mcp_tools()
entry=registry.get_entry('mcp_composio_COMPOSIO_MULTI_EXECUTE_TOOL')
args={
 'tools':[
   {'tool_slug':'GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST','arguments':{'calendarId':'primary','timeMin':'2026-05-21T12:00:00-05:00','timeMax':'2026-05-21T18:00:00-05:00','timeZone':'America/Chicago','singleEvents':True,'orderBy':'startTime','showDeleted':False,'maxResults':20}},
   {'tool_slug':'GOOGLECALENDAR_EVENTS_LIST','arguments':{'calendarId':'en.usa#holiday@group.v.calendar.google.com','timeMin':'2026-05-21T12:00:00-05:00','timeMax':'2026-05-21T18:00:00-05:00','timeZone':'America/Chicago','singleEvents':True,'orderBy':'startTime','showDeleted':False,'maxResults':20}}
 ],
 'thought':'Check selected Google calendars for events this afternoon in America/Chicago.',
 'current_step':'FETCHING_CALENDAR_EVENTS',
 'current_step_metric':'2/2 calendars',
 'sync_response_to_workbench':False
}
out=entry.handler(args)
print(out[:30000])
"}
